label: Decarbonization Technology Evaluation Framework
description: Comprehensive evaluation across technology overview, supply chain position, technical performance, environmental, energy, economic, deployment, and evidence dimensions.
questions: What is the name of the technology?
What category does the technology belong to? Use the predefined technology taxonomy established in the project documentation.
What organization(s), company(ies), university(ies), or research institution(s) developed the technology?
What is the current deployment stage (Laboratory, Pilot, Demonstration, or Commercial)?

Where in the concrete value chain does the technology act? Consider material-side interventions (binder, aggregate, admixture) and demand-side interventions (reduces cement demand, reduces concrete demand, structural efficiency, or other).
How does the technology work, and what conventional material, process, or system does it replace, supplement, or improve?
What quantitative performance improvements are reported for CO₂ reduction, cost reduction, and energy reduction (including reported percentages and baselines used for comparison)?

What additional benefits or performance impacts are reported (e.g., strength improvement, durability, freeze-thaw resistance, sulfate resistance, chloride resistance, resource efficiency, water reduction, faster curing, improved workability, extended service life, or other)?

What is the reported greenhouse gas performance, including emissions reduction (%), absolute emissions intensity, and functional unit (e.g., kg CO₂e per tonne binder, per tonne aggregate, per m³ concrete, per structural element, or per structure)?

What lifecycle boundary or system boundary is used (cradle-to-gate, cradle-to-grave, gate-to-gate, or other), and at what level of analysis (material level: binder/aggregate/admixture; mix level: concrete mix and 28-day strength; element level: foundation/slab/beam/column/other; or structural level: structure type and scale)?
Does the technology increase emissions elsewhere in the supply chain, and are any burden-shifting effects reported?
What assumptions were used in the environmental analysis, and what baseline scenario was used?
What is the energy requirement of the technology, and in what units is it reported?
What type(s) of energy are required (electricity, heat, steam, natural gas, renewable electricity, renewable thermal energy, or other)?
Does the technology increase or decrease total energy demand relative to the conventional approach?
What capital cost (CAPEX) is required?
What operating cost (OPEX) is reported?
What is the cost premium or cost savings relative to the conventional approach?
What assumptions were used in the economic analysis, and what baseline scenario was used?
Can the technology be implemented using existing infrastructure, and what modifications are required?
What are the primary barriers to adoption (technical, regulatory, market, supply chain, capital cost, standards/specifications, or other)?
What geographic, feedstock, or supply-chain resource constraints exist?
Has the technology been deployed commercially? If yes, where and at what scale?

What evidence supports the reported claims (peer-reviewed paper, technical report, environmental product declaration, company whitepaper, pilot project, demonstration project, commercial deployment, or other)?
What is the confidence level in the reported claims (High, Medium, or Low)?
What information is missing, what assumptions remain uncertain, and what data would be needed to improve confidence?
